metadata.title: Title V Permitting for Cement Plants

description: Title V operating permit requirements, compliance obligations, deviation reporting, and permit modification processes for cement manufacturing facilities.

title_v_overview.authority: Clean Air Act Title V (40 CFR Part 70 - federal, Part 71 - federal implementation)
title_v_overview.applicability: Major sources of criteria pollutants (>100 tpy for attainment areas, lower thresholds for nonattainment) or HAPs (>10 tpy single HAP or >25 tpy combined HAPs)
title_v_overview.cement_plant_status: Virtually all cement plants are Title V major sources due to NOx, SO2, and PM emissions

permit_contents.emission_limits: All applicable federal, state, and local emission limits consolidated in one permit
permit_contents.monitoring_requirements: CEMS, stack testing, parametric monitoring, and recordkeeping requirements
permit_contents.reporting_requirements: Quarterly and annual compliance reports, deviation reports, excess emissions reports
permit_contents.compliance_schedule: Timeline for achieving compliance with any new requirements
permit_contents.general_provisions: Permit duration (5 years), renewal process, fee requirements, permit shield provisions

annual_certification.requirement: Responsible official must certify compliance status with all permit terms annually
annual_certification.content: Identify each permit term, state compliance status (continuous compliance, intermittent compliance, or deviation), describe any deviations
annual_certification.due_date: Per permit schedule, typically within 60-90 days of anniversary or calendar year end
annual_certification.signature: Must be signed by responsible official (plant manager or corporate officer with authority)

deviation_reporting.definition: Any departure from a permit condition, including emission limit exceedance, monitoring failure, or failure to meet an operating parameter

prompt_reporting.timeframe: Typically within 2-10 days of discovery (varies by state and severity)
prompt_reporting.content: Date/time, description of deviation, cause, corrective action taken, duration

semi_annual_reporting.requirement: Comprehensive deviation report submitted every 6 months
semi_annual_reporting.content: All deviations during the period, whether previously reported or not

criteria: Event caused by factors beyond reasonable control
Event was not caused by operator negligence or poor maintenance
All reasonable steps were taken to minimize emissions
emergency_provisions.notes: Emergency defense does not excuse the violation but may mitigate penalties

minor_modification.triggers: Changes that do not increase emissions beyond permitted levels or add new regulated pollutants
minor_modification.process: Application to permitting authority, typically 30-60 day review

significant_modification.triggers: Changes that establish or revise emission limits, add emission sources, or change monitoring methods
significant_modification.process: Public notice and comment period, EPA review, typically 6-18 months

administrative_amendment.triggers: Corrections of typographical errors, change of ownership information, similar administrative updates
administrative_amendment.process: Typically 60-day notification, no public review needed

permit_renewal.timeline: Submit renewal application at least 12 months before expiration (varies by state)
permit_renewal.application_shield: Timely and complete renewal application allows continued operation under expired permit terms
permit_renewal.content: Updated emission inventory, compliance history, any proposed changes
